scope_boundary: Artifact Graph receipt-faithfulness adversarial fixture only. The fixture accepts three bounded receipt records and rejects six expected-invalid controls for shape-valid reality-false receipts, ignored trap-receipt failures, missing independent cross-check routes, same-component self-check laundering, unbounded attestation, and support promotion from receipt shape. This is not open-world receipt truth, not verifier-independence evidence, not attestation-service correctness evidence, not deployed audit behavior, not external review, and not an upward support-state transition.
transition_reason: The receipt-faithfulness adversarial fixture is useful because it makes the core record-reality objection executable: a receipt can satisfy record shape while failing to correspond to the artifact or observation it claims to record. The fixture keeps cross-checked, attestation-limited, and trap-detected records inside bounded record-only roles while rejecting receipt-shape support promotion. The evidence remains a finite local fixture, so this accepted decision blocks promotion for open-world receipt faithfulness, verifier independence, attestation-service correctness, deployed audit behavior, benchmark performance, model quality, safety, ASI, or the Artifact Graphs chapter core claim.
required_artifacts: deployed or externally reviewable receipt-faithfulness trace
independent observation route outside the producing component
trap receipt or challenge source outside the producing component
public-safe produced artifact with parent job, source/context set, context transaction, semantic certificate, replay declaration, residual declaration, evidence gate, and claim/test link
deployed or externally reviewable audit behavior
source-interpretation adequacy review where source-derived claims are involved
accepted evidence-transition record for any narrowed upward support movement
negative_results: shape-valid but reality-false receipt promotion is rejected
missing independent cross-check route is rejected
support promotion from receipt shape is rejected
finite fixture evidence remains bounded to synthetic receipt cases
no chapter core claim is promoted
downgrade_triggers: shape-valid reality-false receipt, trap failure, missing independent check, same-component self-check, unbounded attestation, or support-promotion control accepted as valid

finite receipt-faithfulness fixture described as open-world receipt truth, verifier independence, attestation-service correctness, deployed audit behavior, benchmark, model-quality, safety, ASI, or chapter-core evidence
support-state promotion without a separate accepted upward transition
promotion_burden: Move above argument only for a narrowed receipt-faithfulness claim after deployed or externally reviewable receipt traces, independent observer routes outside the producing component, trap/challenge sources outside the producing component, verifier-independence evidence, attestation-limit review, provenance-completeness review, audit-behavior evidence, source-interpretation review where applicable, residuals, non-claims, and independent review are recorded with a separate accepted transition.

does not promote the Artifact Graphs chapter core claim
reviewer_independence: local maintainer-agent review only; not an independent attestation, audit, verifier-independence, source-interpretation, external-project, challenge-source, or external human review

no independent observation route outside the producing component
